{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |English Opening: Agincourt Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | |pd| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |pl| | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = English Opening: Agincourt Defence = ===1...e6=== 1.c4 e6 is the '''Agincourt Defence''' (named for the Battle of Agincourt, fought between the English and the French). Black fights for control of d5 and plans to put a pawn there. Common replies to this opening are 2.Nf3, 2.Nc3, and 2.g3. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}
